note: UI separation only for now - this file is publicly served. Real privacy comes with logins (stage 2).

research_prompts.name: Coin a mechanism
research_prompts.prompt: Invent ONE original micro-mechanism for a transformer LM. Constraints: (1) identity at step 0 - zero-init gates/scales so the first forward pass equals baseline; (2) under 30 lines of code; (3) state WHERE it acts (QKV, attention scores, head outputs, residual write, FFN, embedding, norm) and WHAT signal drives it; (4) write the one-sentence hypothesis for WHY it should help; (5) state the kill criterion: what result kills the idea.

research_prompts.name: Screen protocol
research_prompts.prompt: Run the A/B: tiny tier first (seed 42, fixed data), baseline vs mechanism. LIVENESS CHECK: milestone val losses must differ from baseline - bitwise-identical milestones mean your flag is inert. Noise band = ctrl rerun spread (ours: 0.005). A win must clear it by 3x AND survive the next tier before you claim anything.

research_prompts.name: Hypothesis controls
research_prompts.prompt: Your mechanism won the screen. Before celebrating, design 3 controls: (1) GRANULARITY - the coarser version of your mechanism; if it matches yours, your fine structure is doing nothing. (2) INGREDIENT - turn off the input-dependence (or whatever you claim is active); if the win remains, your explanation is wrong. (3) COMPOSITION - combine with a known win; non-additive means fragile. Pre-register the predicted outcome of each BEFORE running.

research_prompts.name: Negative result write-up
research_prompts.prompt: Write the negative honestly: mechanism, exact deltas, what hypothesis it kills, what it leaves open. A measured null at one scale is not a death sentence at another - say which claims your evidence actually supports. Negatives are paper sections, not trash.

research_prompts.name: Scale-transfer check
research_prompts.prompt: Tiny-tier wins are hypotheses about scale, never claims. Promote: rerun winner + fresh control at the next tier. Watch for: effect growing (real mechanism), effect vanishing (tier artifact), effect INVERTING (we measured one: residual norm gate -0.0166 tiny, +0.0719 at 10x). Composition non-additivity at the small tier is an early fragility warning.

brainstorm_seeds: Where else does per-channel selectivity help? (It was THE ingredient in our V-gate win)
Why does input-side gating help and output-side hurt? What does that say about what the optimizer already balances?
What signal other than RMS norm could drive an input-dependent gate - and would it survive scale?
Sync period H in DiLoCo as a screening axis: what is the cheapest 2-GPU experiment that says something real?

projects.title: Cheap multiplicative gates
projects.one_liner: Where in a transformer do zero-init multiplicative gates help? Screen, control, scale.
projects.hypothesis: ANSWERED: channel selectivity on V is the one gate that helps (-0.0147 tiny, -0.1075 at 10M); input-dependence explained the residual gate's tiny win but it inverted at scale; output-side gating hurts everywhere.

plan.label: Screen 6 gate mechanisms at tiny tier

findings: Value-channel gate: -0.1075 at 10M tier, effect grows with scale
Residual norm gate: tiny-tier win INVERTED at scale (+0.0719) - measured tier artifact
Per-head scalar V gate exactly null -> channel selectivity is the active ingredient
Output-side gating hurts at both granularities
projects.next_action: Fold overnight 10M/200M record results into final table; regenerate figures; publish.

projects.title: Entropy-gated attention heads
projects.one_liner: Does a head's attention entropy predict its value? (No.)
projects.hypothesis: REFUTED: attenuating diffuse heads is null (-0.0013); amplifying confident heads actively hurts (+0.0140). Attention entropy does not predict head value.
findings: Clean two-experiment negative; published as a what-doesn't-work result
projects.next_action: Closed. Kept as the lesson that motivated hypothesis-driven controls.

projects.title: DiLoCo on 2 GPUs
projects.one_liner: Does DiLoCo-style local SGD match single-GPU training at the 10M tier?
projects.hypothesis: DiLoCo with sync period H in [50, 500] matches single-GPU val loss at equal tokens within the tier noise band, while cutting inter-GPU communication ~100x.

plan.label: Design doc: inner/outer optimizer split against our harness
Paper: decentralized == centralized at 10M, or where it breaks
